explanation: FALSE POSITIVE. The unbounded loop in batchTransfer does not constitute a denial-of-service vulnerability against other users. When an attacker calls batchTransfer with 10,000 entries and hits the block gas limit, the transaction simply reverts — atomically undoing all state changes. EVM transaction isolation guarantees that a reverted transaction leaves zero persistent footprint on contract state. Other users retain full, unimpeded access to batchTransfer with reasonably-sized arrays. The only party harmed is the attacker themselves, who loses gas fees. The claim that 'Normal users can no longer execute batchTransfer at all' is incorrect — subsequent callers are completely unaffected. This is a self-inflicted failure, not a vulnerability against third parties. The triage verdict of 'invalid - not important enough' is correct.
